title: trisetum spicatum subsp. molle
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. molle, also known as soft couch grass or soft hair grass, is a subspecies of the Trisetum spicatum plant, a type of grass native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its saponins and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Trisetum spicatum subsp. molle include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of various infections. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves and stems of the plant.
